description_long: This fence is constructed from five thick, vertically aligned wooden logs, each with a pointed, sharpened top, giving it a rustic and defensive appearance. The logs are unevenly shaped, with visible wood grain textures and natural curvature, suggesting they are roughly hewn from tree trunks. Two horizontal wooden rails, also irregular and branch-like, connect the logs near the top and bottom, providing structural support. One of the vertical logs is slightly angled and protrudes forward, adding to the rugged, hand-built look. The wood is a muted brown with lighter streaks, and the pointed tops are lighter in color, as if freshly cut. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, suitable for use as a simple barrier in parks, gardens, or rustic urban settings.
description: A rustic wooden fence made of five pointed logs connected by two rough horizontal rails, with visible wood grain and a hand-built appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five pointed logs, two horizontal rails, and the angled protruding log.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the logs and the forward-leaning protruding log.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the alignment of the five logs and the connecting rails, with less emphasis on the protruding log.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thickness and the protruding log from a different angle.
